title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
A generator function uses the function* syntax and returns a Generator object. It can pause its own execution with yield and resume it on demand.

Key concepts:
• function* — declares a generator.
• yield — pauses the generator and returns a value.
• .next() — resumes execution until the next yield or return.
• The return value of .next() is { value, done }.
• Generators are iterables — usable in for...of loops.

Use cases:
• Lazy data sequences (infinite sequences without crashing memory).
• Custom iterators.
• Asynchronous flow control (older pattern, mostly replaced by async/await).
